    pub fn kernel_send(_id: u32, _data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), &'static str> { Ok(()) }
    pub fn kernel_recv(_id: u32) -> Option<alloc::vec::Vec<u8>> { None }
}

mod timer { // Temporary stub for timer
    pub static mut TICKS: u64 = 0;
}

mod caps { // Temporary stub for capabilities
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub enum Capability { LogWrite, TimeRead, NetworkAccess, StorageAccess }
}

mod arch { // Temporary stub for arch-specific stuff
    pub mod irq { pub fn register_irq_handler(_irq: u8, _chan: u32) { /* ... */ } }
}

// kernel/syscall.rs

// Define custom error codes as constants for clarity
pub const E_ACC_DENIED: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE; // Represents Access Denied
pub const E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; // Represents Unknown Syscall
pub const E_ERROR: u64 = 1; // General error code
pub const SUCCESS: u64 = 0; // Success code

pub const SYS_LOG: u64 = 0;
pub const SYS_IPC_SEND: u64 = 1;
pub const SYS_IPC_RECV: u64 = 2;
pub const SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN: u64 = 3;
pub const SYS_TIME: u64 = 4;
pub const SYS_IRQ_REGISTER: u64 = 5;
pub const SYS_NET_RX_POLL: u64 = 6;

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn syscall_dispatch(n: u64, a1: u64, a2: u64, a3: u64) -> u64 {
    // In a real kernel, current_task would be retrieved from thread-local storage or scheduler state.
    // For this stub, we'll assume it exists and has capabilities.
    let current_task = crate::task::get_current_task();

    match n {
        SYS_LOG => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED; // Access Denied
            }
            let ptr = a1 as *const u8;
            let len = a2 as usize;
            let msg = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(ptr, len) };
            if let Ok(s) = core::str::from_utf8(msg) {
                crate::console::print("[V-Node Log] ");
                crate::console::print(s);
                crate::console::print("\n");
                SUCCESS
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_SEND => {
            let buf = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(a2 as *const u8, a3 as usize) };
            if crate::ipc::kernel_send(a1 as u32, buf).is_ok() { SUCCESS } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_RECV => {
            // For simplicity, assuming a fixed size buffer for now
            let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
            // In a real kernel, you would check the buffer capacity and return actual length
            // For this stub, we return 0 for no data or E_ERROR if nothing is received
            if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
                // Simulate copying data if there's any
                if !data.is_empty() {
                    unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
                    data.len() as u64
                } else { E_ERROR } // No data received or empty message
            } else { E_ERROR } // IPC channel empty or error
        }
        SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN => {
            crate::task::block_current_on_channel(a1 as u32);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_TIME => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            unsafe { crate::timer::TICKS }
        }
        SYS_IRQ_REGISTER => {
            // arg1: irq_number, arg2: channel_id
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or a more specific IRQ cap
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            // Stub for arch::irq::register_irq_handler
            crate::arch::irq::register_irq_handler(a1 as u8, a2 as u32);
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Registering IRQ ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a1);
            crate::console::print(" for channel ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a2);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_RX_POLL => {
            // arg1: interface_id (from cap), arg2: out_buffer_ptr
            // This would interact with a hardware abstraction layer in the kernel to poll the NIC for packets
            // For now, simulate no packets, returning 0 length to indicate no data
            SUCCESS // Simulate no packets for now (returning 0 length)
        }
        _ => E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL, // Unknown syscall
    }
}
'''

# In a real environment, you would write this string to the actual file:
# with open("kernel/syscall.rs", "w") as f:
#     f.write(rust_code)

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the kernel syscalls for network operations are defined, I will create the Rust code for the `net-bridge` V-Node as requested. This code will utilize the exposed syscalls to register for interrupts and poll for network data, demonstrating the V-Node's interaction with the kernel.


"""

print("Creating vnode/net-bridge.rs code.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SYS_IRQ_REGISTER, SYS_NET_RX_POLL, SUCCESS, E_ERROR};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For this V-Node, let's assume its channel ID is passed by the kernel
    // or is a well-known ID for the net-bridge service.
    // For simplicity, we'll hardcode it to 2 for now, assuming 1 is for registry.
    let mut chan = VNodeChannel::new(2);

    log("Net-Bridge V-Node starting up...");

    // Register IRQ 11 (common for VirtIO-Net) for this V-Node's channel
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_IRQ_REGISTER,
            11 as u64, // IRQ number for VirtIO-Net
            chan.id as u64, // Channel ID to route IRQ events
            0 // arg3 is unused
        );
        if res == SUCCESS {
            log("Net-Bridge: Registered IRQ 11 successfully.");
        } else {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to register IRQ 11.");
            // In a real scenario, handle error: maybe exit or retry
        }
    }

    // Buffer for receiving network packets
    let mut rx_buffer = [0u8; 1536]; // Max Ethernet frame size

    loop {
        // Wait for IRQ events from the kernel (on its own channel)
        // This will block until an IRQ event is routed to this V-Node's channel
        match chan.recv_blocking() {
            Ok(msg_data) => {
                // In a real scenario, msg_data would contain details about the IRQ event
                // For now, we'll just log that an event occurred and proceed to poll
                log("Net-Bridge: Received IRQ event. Polling for packets...");

                // Poll for incoming network packets
                unsafe {
                    let len = syscall3(
                        SYS_NET_RX_POLL,
                        0 as u64, // Interface ID (from cap, assumed 0 for now)
                        rx_buffer.as_mut_ptr() as u64,
                        rx_buffer.len() as u64 // Max buffer length
                    );

                    if len > SUCCESS {
                        log("Net-Bridge: Received packet!");
                        // In a real scenario, process the packet (e.g., send to svc://aethernet)
                        // For now, just log and discard
                    } else if len == SUCCESS {
                        log("Net-Bridge: Poll returned no packets (expected if IRQ was spurious or handled).");
                    } else if len == E_ERROR {
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned an error.");
                    } else {
                        // Other error codes from syscall_dispatch
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned unknown error code.");
                    }
                }
            },
            Err(_) => {
                log("Net-Bridge: Error receiving IPC message on its channel.");
                // Potentially fatal error, depending on error handling strategy
            }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Net-Bridge V-Node panicked!");
    loop {}
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""## Kernel-side NET Syscalls (syscall.rs)

### Subtask:

**Reasoning**:
I need to add the new syscall constants and implement their handling logic in `kernel/syscall.rs`. This requires defining placeholder functions for DMA allocation/freeing, packet transmission, and IRQ acknowledgment within the kernel context. I'll extend the `syscall_dispatch` function with `SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF`, `SYS_NET_FREE_BUF`, `SYS_NET_TX`, and `SYS_IRQ_ACK`, ensuring capability checks and stubbing out the underlying kernel logic for now.
"""

print("Updating kernel/syscall.rs with new syscalls and their handling logic, including stubs for underlying kernel functionality.")

# The content below represents the full Rust code for kernel/syscall.rs, including necessary stubs
# that would typically be defined in other kernel modules and imported. This is enclosed within a Python string.
rust_code = r'''
// For simplicity, assume these modules/functions exist or are stubbed in the kernel context
// In a real kernel, these would be properly defined and imported.
mod console { // Temporary stub for console output
    // In a real kernel, these would write to serial port or framebuffer
    pub fn print(s: &str) { /* ... */ }
    pub fn print_u64(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
    pub fn print_hex(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
}

mod task { // Temporary stub for task management
    pub struct Task;
    impl Task {
        pub fn has_cap(&self, cap: crate::caps::Capability) -> bool {
            // For stub, assume all capabilities are granted if not explicitly checked for testing
            match cap {
                crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess => true, // Allow net access for net-bridge development
                crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite => true,
                crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead => true,
                _ => false // Deny others by default
            }
        }
    }
    pub fn get_current_task() -> Task { Task }
    pub fn block_current_on_channel(_id: u32) { /* ... */ }
}

mod ipc { // Temporary stub for IPC
    extern crate alloc;
    pub fn kernel_send(_id: u32, _data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), &'static str> { Ok(()) }
    pub fn kernel_recv(_id: u32) -> Option<alloc::vec::Vec<u8>> { None } // Returns None for simplicity
}

mod timer { // Temporary stub for timer
    pub static mut TICKS: u64 = 0;
}

mod caps { // Temporary stub for capabilities
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub enum Capability { LogWrite, TimeRead, NetworkAccess, StorageAccess }
}

mod arch { // Temporary stub for arch-specific stuff
    pub mod irq {
        pub fn register_irq_handler(_irq: u8, _chan: u32) {
            // In real impl, would store (irq -> channel_id) mapping
        }
        pub fn acknowledge_irq(_irq: u8) {
            // In real impl, would send EOI to PIC/APIC
        }
    }
    pub mod dma { // Stub for DMA management
        extern crate alloc;
        use alloc::vec::Vec;
        // Simulate DMA buffer allocation
        pub fn alloc_dma_buffer(_size: usize) -> Option<u64> { Some(0x_DEAD_BEEF_0000_0000) } // Return a fake handle
        pub fn free_dma_buffer(_handle: u64) { /* ... */ }
    }
}

// kernel/syscall.rs

// Define custom error codes as constants for clarity
pub const E_ACC_DENIED: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE; // Represents Access Denied
pub const E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; // Represents Unknown Syscall
pub const E_ERROR: u64 = 1; // General error code
pub const SUCCESS: u64 = 0; // Success code

pub const SYS_LOG: u64 = 0;
pub const SYS_IPC_SEND: u64 = 1;
pub const SYS_IPC_RECV: u64 = 2;
pub const SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN: u64 = 3;
pub const SYS_TIME: u64 = 4;
pub const SYS_IRQ_REGISTER: u64 = 5;
pub const SYS_NET_RX_POLL: u64 = 6;
pub const SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF: u64 = 7;
pub const SYS_NET_FREE_BUF: u64 = 8;
pub const SYS_NET_TX: u64 = 9;
pub const SYS_IRQ_ACK: u64 = 10;

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn syscall_dispatch(n: u64, a1: u64, a2: u64, a3: u64) -> u64 {
    // In a real kernel, current_task would be retrieved from thread-local storage or scheduler state.
    // For this stub, we'll assume it exists and has capabilities.
    let current_task = crate::task::get_current_task();

    match n {
        SYS_LOG => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED; // Access Denied
            }
            let ptr = a1 as *const u8;
            let len = a2 as usize;
            let msg = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(ptr, len) };
            if let Ok(s) = core::str::from_utf8(msg) {
                crate::console::print("[V-Node Log] ");
                crate::console::print(s);
                crate::console::print("\n");
                SUCCESS
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_SEND => {
            let buf = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(a2 as *const u8, a3 as usize) };
            if crate::ipc::kernel_send(a1 as u32, buf).is_ok() { SUCCESS } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_RECV => {
            // For simplicity, assuming a fixed size buffer for now
            let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
            // In a real kernel, you would check the buffer capacity and return actual length
            // For this stub, we return 0 for no data or E_ERROR if nothing is received
            if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
                // Simulate copying data if there's any
                if !data.is_empty() {
                    unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
                    data.len() as u64
                } else { E_ERROR } // No data received or empty message
            } else { E_ERROR } // IPC channel empty or error
        }
        SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN => {
            crate::task::block_current_on_channel(a1 as u32);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_TIME => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            unsafe { crate::timer::TICKS }
        }
        SYS_IRQ_REGISTER => {
            // arg1: irq_number, arg2: channel_id
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::irq::register_irq_handler(a1 as u8, a2 as u32);
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Registering IRQ ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a1);
            crate::console::print(" for channel ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a2);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_RX_POLL => {
            // arg1: interface_id (from cap), arg2: out_buffer_ptr, arg3: out_buffer_len
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            // For now, simulate no packets, returning 0 length to indicate no data
            SUCCESS // Simulate no packets for now (returning 0 length)
        }
        SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF => {
            // arg1: size
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ALLOC
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            if let Some(handle) = crate::arch::dma::alloc_dma_buffer(a1 as usize) {
                handle
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_NET_FREE_BUF => {
            // arg1: handle
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ALLOC
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::dma::free_dma_buffer(a1);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_TX => {
            // arg1: interface_id (from cap), arg2: buf_handle, arg3: len
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Queuing packet for TX, handle: ");
            crate::console::print_hex(a2);
            crate::console::print(", len: ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a3);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            // In a real kernel, this would interact with the NIC's transmit ring
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_IRQ_ACK => {
            // arg1: irq_number
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or specific IRQ cap
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::irq::acknowledge_irq(a1 as u8);
            SUCCESS
        }
        _ => E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL, // Unknown syscall
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the kernel syscalls for network operations are defined, I will create the Rust code for the `net-bridge` V-Node as requested. This code will utilize the exposed syscalls to register for interrupts and poll for network data, demonstrating the V-Node's interaction with the kernel.


"""

print("Creating vnode/net-bridge.rs code.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SYS_IRQ_REGISTER, SYS_NET_RX_POLL, SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, SYS_NET_TX, SYS_IRQ_ACK, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, E_ACC_DENIED};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for allocating a DMA buffer
fn net_alloc_buf(size: usize) -> Result<u64, u64> {
    unsafe {
        let handle = syscall2(SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, size as u64, 0);
        if handle == E_ERROR || handle == E_ACC_DENIED { Err(handle) } else { Ok(handle) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for freeing a DMA buffer
fn net_free_buf(handle: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, handle, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for transmitting a network packet
fn net_tx(iface_id: u64, buf_handle: u64, len: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(SYS_NET_TX, iface_id, buf_handle, len);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for acknowledging an IRQ
fn irq_ack(irq_num: u8) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_IRQ_ACK, irq_num as u64, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For this V-Node, let's assume its channel ID is passed by the kernel
    // or is a well-known ID for the net-bridge service.
    // For simplicity, we'll hardcode it to 2 for now, assuming 1 is for registry.
    let mut chan = VNodeChannel::new(2);

    log("Net-Bridge V-Node starting up...");

    // Register IRQ 11 (common for VirtIO-Net) for this V-Node's channel
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_IRQ_REGISTER,
            11 as u64, // IRQ number for VirtIO-Net
            chan.id as u64, // Channel ID to route IRQ events
            0 // arg3 is unused
        );
        if res == SUCCESS {
            log("Net-Bridge: Registered IRQ 11 successfully.");
        } else {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to register IRQ 11.");
            // In a real scenario, handle error: maybe exit or retry
        }
    }

    // Allocate a DMA buffer for receiving network packets
    let rx_buf_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
        Ok(h) => {
            log("Net-Bridge: Allocated RX buffer.");
            h
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to allocate RX buffer.");
            // In a real scenario, panic or exit
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    loop {
        // Wait for IRQ events from the kernel (on its own channel)
        // This will block until an IRQ event is routed to this V-Node's channel
        match chan.recv_blocking() {
            Ok(msg_data) => {
                // In a real scenario, msg_data would contain details about the IRQ event
                // For now, we'll just log that an event occurred and proceed to poll
                log("Net-Bridge: Received IRQ event. Polling for packets...");

                // Poll for incoming network packets
                unsafe {
                    // SYS_NET_RX_POLL expects a handle to a pre-allocated buffer
                    let len = syscall3(
                        SYS_NET_RX_POLL,
                        0 as u64, // Interface ID (from cap, assumed 0 for now)
                        rx_buf_handle, // Pass the allocated buffer handle
                        1536 as u64 // Max buffer length
                    );

                    if len > SUCCESS {
                        log("Net-Bridge: Received packet!");
                        // In a real scenario, process the packet (e.g., send to svc://aethernet)
                        // For now, just log and discard

                        // Acknowledge the IRQ after processing
                        if irq_ack(11).is_err() {
                            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to acknowledge IRQ 11.");
                        }
                    } else if len == SUCCESS { // len == 0, no packet (spurious IRQ or already handled)
                        log("Net-Bridge: Poll returned no packets (expected if IRQ was spurious or handled).");
                         if irq_ack(11).is_err() {
                            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to acknowledge IRQ 11.");
                        }
                    } else if len == E_ERROR {
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned an error.");
                    } else {
                        // Other error codes from syscall_dispatch
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned unknown error code.");
                    }
                }
            },
            Err(_) => {
                log("Net-Bridge: Error receiving IPC message on its channel.");
                // Potentially fatal error, depending on error handling strategy
            }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Net-Bridge V-Node panicked!");
    // In a real system, attempt to free allocated resources like rx_buf_handle
    loop {}
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""## Implement Simulated RX/TX for net-bridge

### Subtask:
Implement simulated network packet reception and transmission in the kernel, and integrate it into the `net-bridge` V-Node.

**Reasoning**:
The user wants to implement simulated network packet reception and transmission. This requires updating the kernel-side syscalls first to provide the necessary functionality for `net-bridge` to interact with. I will update `kernel/syscall.rs` to simulate packet reception and DMA management, and refine the `arch::dma` and `arch::irq` stubs.
"""

print("Updating kernel/syscall.rs with simulated RX/TX and DMA functionality, along with updated stubs.")

# The content below represents the full Rust code for kernel/syscall.rs, including necessary stubs
# that would typically be defined in other kernel modules and imported. This is enclosed within a Python string.
rust_code = r'''
// For simplicity, assume these modules/functions exist or are stubbed in the kernel context
// In a real kernel, these would be properly defined and imported.

extern crate alloc;
use alloc::vec::Vec;

mod console { // Temporary stub for console output
    // In a real kernel, these would write to serial port or framebuffer
    pub fn print(s: &str) { /* ... */ }
    pub fn print_u64(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
    pub fn print_hex(n: u64) { /* ... */ }
}

mod task { // Temporary stub for task management
    pub struct Task;
    impl Task {
        pub fn has_cap(&self, cap: crate::caps::Capability) -> bool {
            // For stub, assume all capabilities are granted if not explicitly checked for testing
            match cap {
                crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess => true, // Allow net access for net-bridge development
                crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite => true,
                crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead => true,
                _ => false // Deny others by default
            }
        }
    }
    pub fn get_current_task() -> Task { Task }
    pub fn block_current_on_channel(_id: u32) { /* ... */ }
}

mod ipc { // Temporary stub for IPC
    extern crate alloc;
    pub fn kernel_send(_id: u32, _data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), &'static str> { Ok(()) }
    pub fn kernel_recv(_id: u32) -> Option<alloc::vec::Vec<u8>> { None } // Returns None for simplicity
}

mod timer { // Temporary stub for timer
    pub static mut TICKS: u64 = 0;
}

mod caps { // Temporary stub for capabilities
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub enum Capability { LogWrite, TimeRead, NetworkAccess, StorageAccess }
}

mod arch { // Temporary stub for arch-specific stuff
    pub mod irq {
        pub fn register_irq_handler(_irq: u8, _chan: u32) {
            // In real impl, would store (irq -> channel_id) mapping
            // For now, just log
            crate::console::print("[Kernel IRQ Stub] Registered IRQ ");
            crate::console::print_u64(_irq as u64);
            crate::console::print(" to channel ");
            crate::console::print_u64(_chan as u64);
            crate::console::print("\n");
        }
        pub fn acknowledge_irq(_irq: u8) {
            // In real impl, would send EOI to PIC/APIC
            crate::console::print("[Kernel IRQ Stub] Acknowledged IRQ ");
            crate::console::print_u64(_irq as u64);
            crate::console::print("\n");
        }
    }
    pub mod dma { // Stub for DMA management
        extern crate alloc;
        use alloc::collections::BTreeMap;
        use spin::Mutex;
        use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};

        // Simulate a simple allocator for DMA buffers
        static NEXT_HANDLE: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(1);
        static DMA_BUFFERS: Mutex<BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u8>>> = Mutex::new(BTreeMap::new());

        pub fn alloc_dma_buffer(size: usize) -> Option<u64> {
            let handle = NEXT_HANDLE.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            let buffer = Vec::with_capacity(size); // Allocate a Vec as a placeholder for a DMA page
            buffers.insert(handle, buffer);
            crate::console::print("[Kernel DMA Stub] Allocated DMA buffer with handle ");
            crate::console::print_hex(handle);
            crate::console::print(" and size ");
            crate::console::print_u64(size as u64);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            Some(handle)
        }

        pub fn free_dma_buffer(handle: u64) {
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            buffers.remove(&handle);
            crate::console::print("[Kernel DMA Stub] Freed DMA buffer with handle ");
            crate::console::print_hex(handle);
            crate::console::print("\n");
        }

        // Simulate getting a pointer to the DMA buffer data
        pub fn get_dma_buffer_ptr(handle: u64) -> Option<*mut u8> {
            let buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            buffers.get(&handle).map(|buf| buf.as_ptr() as *mut u8)
        }

        // Simulate getting a mutable reference to the DMA buffer data
        pub fn get_dma_buffer_mut(handle: u64) -> Option<&'static mut Vec<u8>> {
            // WARNING: This is highly unsafe in a real kernel as it breaks Rust's aliasing rules.
            // In a real kernel, DMA buffers are typically mapped to virtual addresses
            // and then accessed. This stub directly exposes the Vec.
            let mut buffers = DMA_BUFFERS.lock();
            buffers.get_mut(&handle).map(|buf| unsafe { core::mem::transmute(buf) })
        }


    }
}

// kernel/syscall.rs

// Define custom error codes as constants for clarity
pub const E_ACC_DENIED: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE; // Represents Access Denied
pub const E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL: u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; // Represents Unknown Syscall
pub const E_ERROR: u64 = 1; // General error code
pub const SUCCESS: u64 = 0; // Success code

pub const SYS_LOG: u64 = 0;
pub const SYS_IPC_SEND: u64 = 1;
pub const SYS_IPC_RECV: u64 = 2;
pub const SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN: u64 = 3;
pub const SYS_TIME: u64 = 4;
pub const SYS_IRQ_REGISTER: u64 = 5;
pub const SYS_NET_RX_POLL: u64 = 6;
pub const SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF: u64 = 7;
pub const SYS_NET_FREE_BUF: u64 = 8;
pub const SYS_NET_TX: u64 = 9;
pub const SYS_IRQ_ACK: u64 = 10;

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn syscall_dispatch(n: u64, a1: u64, a2: u64, a3: u64) -> u64 {
    // In a real kernel, current_task would be retrieved from thread-local storage or scheduler state.
    // For this stub, we'll assume it exists and has capabilities.
    let current_task = crate::task::get_current_task();

    match n {
        SYS_LOG => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::LogWrite) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED; // Access Denied
            }
            let ptr = a1 as *const u8;
            let len = a2 as usize;
            let msg = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(ptr, len) };
            if let Ok(s) = core::str::from_utf8(msg) {
                crate::console::print("[V-Node Log] ");
                crate::console::print(s);
                crate::console::print("\n");
                SUCCESS
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_SEND => {
            let buf = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(a2 as *const u8, a3 as usize) };
            if crate::ipc::kernel_send(a1 as u32, buf).is_ok() { SUCCESS } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_IPC_RECV => {
            // For simplicity, assuming a fixed size buffer for now
            let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
            // In a real kernel, you would check the buffer capacity and return actual length
            // For this stub, we return 0 for no data or E_ERROR if nothing is received
            if let Some(data) = crate::ipc::kernel_recv(a1 as u32) {
                // Simulate copying data if there's any
                if !data.is_empty() {
                    unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(data.as_ptr(), out_ptr, data.len()); }
                    data.len() as u64
                } else { E_ERROR } // No data received or empty message
            } else { E_ERROR } // IPC channel empty or error
        }
        SYS_BLOCK_ON_CHAN => {
            crate::task::block_current_on_channel(a1 as u32);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_TIME => {
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::TimeRead) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            unsafe { crate::timer::TICKS }
        }
        SYS_IRQ_REGISTER => {
            // arg1: irq_number, arg2: channel_id
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or a more specific IRQ cap
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::irq::register_irq_handler(a1 as u8, a2 as u32);
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Registering IRQ ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a1);
            crate::console::print(" for channel ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a2);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_RX_POLL => {
            // arg1: interface_id (from cap), arg2: out_buffer_ptr, arg3: out_buffer_len
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            // Simulate a hardcoded Ethernet packet for reception
            let simulated_packet: [u8; 42] = [
                0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, // Dest MAC: Broadcast
                0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, // Src MAC: Dummy
                0x08, 0x06,                         // EtherType: ARP
                0x00, 0x01, 0x08, 0x00, 0x06, 0x04, // ARP Header
                0x00, 0x01,                         // Opcode: Request
                0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, // Sender MAC
                0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x01,             // Sender IP: 192.168.1.1
                0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Target MAC
                0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x02,             // Target IP: 192.168.1.2
            ];
            let packet_len = simulated_packet.len();
            let out_ptr = a2 as *mut u8;
            let out_len_cap = a3 as usize;

            if packet_len <= out_len_cap {
                unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(simulated_packet.as_ptr(), out_ptr, packet_len); }
                packet_len as u64
            } else {
                E_ERROR // Buffer too small
            }
        }
        SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF => {
            // arg1: size
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ALLOC
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            if let Some(handle) = crate::arch::dma::alloc_dma_buffer(a1 as usize) {
                handle
            } else { E_ERROR }
        }
        SYS_NET_FREE_BUF => {
            // arg1: handle
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or CAP_DMA_ALLOC
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::dma::free_dma_buffer(a1);
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_NET_TX => {
            // arg1: interface_id (from cap), arg2: buf_handle, arg3: len
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) {
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::console::print("[Kernel] Queuing packet for TX, handle: ");
            crate::console::print_hex(a2);
            crate::console::print(", len: ");
            crate::console::print_u64(a3);
            crate::console::print("\n");
            // In a real kernel, this would interact with the NIC's transmit ring
            SUCCESS
        }
        SYS_IRQ_ACK => {
            // arg1: irq_number
            if !current_task.has_cap(crate::caps::Capability::NetworkAccess) { // Or specific IRQ cap
                return E_ACC_DENIED;
            }
            crate::arch::irq::acknowledge_irq(a1 as u8);
            SUCCESS
        }
        _ => E_UNKNOWN_SYSCALL, // Unknown syscall
    }
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
Now that the kernel syscalls for network operations are defined and simulated, I need to complete the `net-bridge` V-Node code to demonstrate both receiving and transmitting simulated network packets, as per the instructions. This involves allocating a buffer, filling it with test data, transmitting it, and then freeing the buffer.


"""

print("Creating vnode/net-bridge.rs code.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SYS_IRQ_REGISTER, SYS_NET_RX_POLL, SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, SYS_NET_TX, SYS_IRQ_ACK, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, E_ACC_DENIED};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for allocating a DMA buffer
fn net_alloc_buf(size: usize) -> Result<u64, u64> {
    unsafe {
        let handle = syscall2(SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, size as u64, 0);
        if handle == E_ERROR || handle == E_ACC_DENIED { Err(handle) } else { Ok(handle) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for freeing a DMA buffer
fn net_free_buf(handle: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, handle, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for transmitting a network packet
fn net_tx(iface_id: u64, buf_handle: u64, len: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(SYS_NET_TX, iface_id, buf_handle, len);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for acknowledging an IRQ
fn irq_ack(irq_num: u8) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_IRQ_ACK, irq_num as u64, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For this V-Node, let's assume its channel ID is passed by the kernel
    // or is a well-known ID for the net-bridge service.
    // For simplicity, we'll hardcode it to 2 for now, assuming 1 is for registry.
    let mut chan = VNodeChannel::new(2);

    log("Net-Bridge V-Node starting up...");

    // Register IRQ 11 (common for VirtIO-Net) for this V-Node's channel
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_IRQ_REGISTER,
            11 as u64, // IRQ number for VirtIO-Net
            chan.id as u64, // Channel ID to route IRQ events
            0 // arg3 is unused
        );
        if res == SUCCESS {
            log("Net-Bridge: Registered IRQ 11 successfully.");
        } else {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to register IRQ 11.");
            // In a real scenario, handle error: maybe exit or retry
        }
    }

    // Allocate a DMA buffer for receiving network packets
    let rx_buf_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
        Ok(h) => {
            log("Net-Bridge: Allocated RX buffer.");
            h
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to allocate RX buffer.");
            // In a real scenario, panic or exit
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    loop {
        // Wait for IRQ events from the kernel (on its own channel)
        // This will block until an IRQ event is routed to this V-Node's channel
        match chan.recv_blocking() {
            Ok(msg_data) => {
                // In a real scenario, msg_data would contain details about the IRQ event
                // For now, we'll just log that an event occurred and proceed to poll
                log("Net-Bridge: Received IRQ event. Polling for packets...");

                // Poll for incoming network packets
                unsafe {
                    // SYS_NET_RX_POLL expects a handle to a pre-allocated buffer
                    let len = syscall3(
                        SYS_NET_RX_POLL,
                        0 as u64, // Interface ID (from cap, assumed 0 for now)
                        rx_buf_handle, // Pass the allocated buffer handle
                        1536 as u64 // Max buffer length
                    );

                    if len > SUCCESS {
                        log("Net-Bridge: Received packet!");
                        // In a real scenario, process the packet (e.g., send to svc://aethernet)
                        // For now, just log and discard

                        // Acknowledge the IRQ after processing
                        if irq_ack(11).is_err() {
                            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to acknowledge IRQ 11.");
                        }
                    } else if len == SUCCESS { // len == 0, no packet (spurious IRQ or already handled)
                        log("Net-Bridge: Poll returned no packets (expected if IRQ was spurious or handled).");
                         if irq_ack(11).is_err() {
                            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to acknowledge IRQ 11.");
                        }
                    } else if len == E_ERROR {
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned an error.");
                    } else {
                        // Other error codes from syscall_dispatch
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned unknown error code.");
                    }
                }

                // --- Simulate TX ---
                // Allocate a DMA buffer for a test packet to transmit
                let tx_buf_handle = match net_alloc_buf(60) { // Min Ethernet frame size
                    Ok(h) => h,
                    Err(_) => { log("Net-Bridge: Failed to allocate TX buffer."); continue; }
                };

                // Get a mutable pointer to the allocated DMA buffer
                let tx_ptr = crate::syscall::get_dma_buffer_ptr(tx_buf_handle) as *mut u8;
                if tx_ptr.is_null() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to get TX buffer pointer.");
                    net_free_buf(tx_buf_handle);
                    continue;
                }

                // Sample Ethernet frame data (e.g., a simple ARP request)
                let sample_packet: [u8; 60] = [
                    0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, // Dest MAC: Broadcast
                    0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, // Src MAC: Test
                    0x08, 0x06,                         // EtherType: ARP
                    0x00, 0x01, 0x08, 0x00, 0x06, 0x04, // ARP Header
                    0x00, 0x01,                         // Opcode: Request
                    0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, // Sender MAC
                    0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x03,             // Sender IP: 192.168.1.3
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Target MAC
                    0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x04,             // Target IP: 192.168.1.4
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Padding
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00,
                ];

                // Copy the sample packet data into the DMA buffer
                unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(sample_packet.as_ptr(), tx_ptr, sample_packet.len()); }

                // Transmit the packet
                if net_tx(0, tx_buf_handle, sample_packet.len() as u64).is_ok() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Transmitted packet!");
                } else {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to transmit packet.");
                }

                // Free the allocated DMA buffer after transmission
                if net_free_buf(tx_buf_handle).is_err() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to free TX buffer.");
                }
                // --- End Simulate TX ---

            },
            Err(_) => {
                log("Net-Bridge: Error receiving IPC message on its channel.");
                // Potentially fatal error, depending on error handling strategy
            }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Net-Bridge V-Node panicked!");
    // In a real system, attempt to free allocated resources like rx_buf_handle
    loop {}
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""**Reasoning**:
The kernel syscalls for network operations are now defined and simulated. The next step is to provide the `net-bridge` V-Node code that utilizes these syscalls to register for IRQs, allocate DMA buffers for RX, poll for simulated incoming packets, allocate a DMA buffer for TX, fill it with sample data, transmit it, and then free the TX DMA buffer. This completes the simulated RX/TX cycle for the `net-bridge` V-Node.


"""

print("Creating vnode/net-bridge.rs code.")

            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );

    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_IRQ_ACK, irq_num as u64, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For this V-Node, let's assume its channel ID is passed by the kernel
    // or is a well-known ID for the net-bridge service.
    // For simplicity, we'll hardcode it to 2 for now, assuming 1 is for registry.

        // This will block until an IRQ event is routed to this V-Node's channel

                        0 as u64, // Interface ID (from cap, assumed 0 for now)
                        rx_buf_handle, // Pass the allocated buffer handle
                        1536 as u64 // Max buffer length
                    );

                    if len > SUCCESS {

                        // For now, just log and discard

                        // Acknowledge the IRQ after processing
                        if irq_ack(11).is_err() {

                        log("Net-Bridge: Poll returned no packets (expected if IRQ was spurious or handled).");
                         if irq_ack(11).is_err() {

                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned an error.");

                    }

                let tx_buf_handle = match net_alloc_buf(60) { // Min Ethernet frame size

                };

                if tx_ptr.is_null() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to get TX buffer pointer.");
                    net_free_buf(tx_buf_handle);
                    continue;
                }

                // Sample Ethernet frame data (e.g., a simple ARP request)
                let sample_packet: [u8; 60] = [
                    0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, // Dest MAC: Broadcast
                    0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, // Src MAC: Test
                    0x08, 0x06,                         // EtherType: ARP
                    0x00, 0x01, 0x08, 0x00, 0x06, 0x04, // ARP Header
                    0x00, 0x01,                         // Opcode: Request
                    0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, // Sender MAC
                    0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x03,             // Sender IP: 192.168.1.3
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Target MAC
                    0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x04,             // Target IP: 192.168.1.4
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Padding
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00,
                ];

                // Copy the sample packet data into the DMA buffer
                unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(sample_packet.as_ptr(), tx_ptr, sample_packet.len()); }

                // Transmit the packet
                if net_tx(0, tx_buf_handle, sample_packet.len() as u64).is_ok() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Transmitted packet!");
                } else {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to transmit packet.");
                }

                // Free the allocated DMA buffer after transmission
                if net_free_buf(tx_buf_handle).is_err() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to free TX buffer.");
                }
                // --- End Simulate TX ---

            },
            Err(_) => {
                log("Net-Bridge: Error receiving IPC message on its channel.");
                // Potentially fatal error, depending on error handling strategy
            }
        }
    }
}

    // In a real system, attempt to free allocated resources like rx_buf_handle
    loop {}

"""**Reasoning**:
The kernel syscalls for network operations are now defined and simulated. The provided `net-bridge` V-Node code demonstrates the full simulated RX/TX cycle by registering IRQs, allocating DMA buffers, polling for incoming packets, transmitting a simulated packet, and freeing resources.


"""

print("Creating vnode/net-bridge.rs code.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SYS_IRQ_REGISTER, SYS_NET_RX_POLL, SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, SYS_NET_TX, SYS_IRQ_ACK, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, E_ACC_DENIED};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for allocating a DMA buffer
fn net_alloc_buf(size: usize) -> Result<u64, u64> {
    unsafe {
        let handle = syscall2(SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, size as u64, 0);
        if handle == E_ERROR || handle == E_ACC_DENIED { Err(handle) } else { Ok(handle) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for freeing a DMA buffer
fn net_free_buf(handle: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, handle, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for transmitting a network packet
fn net_tx(iface_id: u64, buf_handle: u64, len: u64) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(SYS_NET_TX, iface_id, buf_handle, len);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

// Temporary syscall for acknowledging an IRQ
fn irq_ack(irq_num: u8) -> Result<(), u64> {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall2(SYS_IRQ_ACK, irq_num as u64, 0);
        if res != SUCCESS { Err(res) } else { Ok(()) }
    }
}

#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For this V-Node, let's assume its channel ID is passed by the kernel
    // or is a well-known ID for the net-bridge service.
    // For simplicity, we'll hardcode it to 2 for now, assuming 1 is for registry.
    let mut chan = VNodeChannel::new(2);

    log("Net-Bridge V-Node starting up...");

    // Register IRQ 11 (common for VirtIO-Net) for this V-Node's channel
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_IRQ_REGISTER,
            11 as u64, // IRQ number for VirtIO-Net
            chan.id as u64, // Channel ID to route IRQ events
            0 // arg3 is unused
        );
        if res == SUCCESS {
            log("Net-Bridge: Registered IRQ 11 successfully.");
        } else {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to register IRQ 11.");
            // In a real scenario, handle error: maybe exit or retry
        }
    }

    // Allocate a DMA buffer for receiving network packets
    let rx_buf_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
        Ok(h) => {
            log("Net-Bridge: Allocated RX buffer.");
            h
        },
        Err(e) => {
            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to allocate RX buffer.");
            // In a real scenario, panic or exit
            loop { /* ... */ }
        }
    };

    loop {
        // Wait for IRQ events from the kernel (on its own channel)
        // This will block until an IRQ event is routed to this V-Node's channel
        match chan.recv_blocking() {
            Ok(msg_data) => {
                // In a real scenario, msg_data would contain details about the IRQ event
                // For now, we'll just log that an event occurred and proceed to poll
                log("Net-Bridge: Received IRQ event. Polling for packets...");

                // Poll for incoming network packets
                unsafe {
                    // SYS_NET_RX_POLL expects a handle to a pre-allocated buffer
                    let len = syscall3(
                        SYS_NET_RX_POLL,
                        0 as u64, // Interface ID (from cap, assumed 0 for now)
                        rx_buf_handle, // Pass the allocated buffer handle
                        1536 as u64 // Max buffer length
                    );

                    if len > SUCCESS {
                        log("Net-Bridge: Received packet!");
                        // In a real scenario, process the packet (e.g., send to svc://aethernet)
                        // For now, just log and discard

                        // Acknowledge the IRQ after processing
                        if irq_ack(11).is_err() {
                            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to acknowledge IRQ 11.");
                        }
                    } else if len == SUCCESS { // len == 0, no packet (spurious IRQ or already handled)
                        log("Net-Bridge: Poll returned no packets (expected if IRQ was spurious or handled).");
                         if irq_ack(11).is_err() {
                            log("Net-Bridge: Failed to acknowledge IRQ 11.");
                        }
                    } else if len == E_ERROR {
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned an error.");
                    }
                    else {
                        // Other error codes from syscall_dispatch
                        log("Net-Bridge: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned unknown error code.");
                    }
                }

                // --- Simulate TX ---
                // Allocate a DMA buffer for a test packet to transmit
                let tx_buf_handle = match net_alloc_buf(60) { // Min Ethernet frame size
                    Ok(h) => h,
                    Err(_) => { log("Net-Bridge: Failed to allocate TX buffer."); continue; }
                };

                // Get a mutable pointer to the allocated DMA buffer
                let tx_ptr = crate::syscall::get_dma_buffer_ptr(tx_buf_handle) as *mut u8;
                if tx_ptr.is_null() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to get TX buffer pointer.");
                    net_free_buf(tx_buf_handle);
                    continue;
                }

                // Sample Ethernet frame data (e.g., a simple ARP request)
                let sample_packet: [u8; 60] = [
                    0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, // Dest MAC: Broadcast
                    0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, // Src MAC: Test
                    0x08, 0x06,                         // EtherType: ARP
                    0x00, 0x01, 0x08, 0x00, 0x06, 0x04, // ARP Header
                    0x00, 0x01,                         // Opcode: Request
                    0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, // Sender MAC
                    0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x03,             // Sender IP: 192.168.1.3
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Target MAC
                    0xC0, 0xA8, 0x01, 0x04,             // Target IP: 192.168.1.4
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, // Padding
                    0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00,
                ];

                // Copy the sample packet data into the DMA buffer
                unsafe { core::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(sample_packet.as_ptr(), tx_ptr, sample_packet.len()); }

                // Transmit the packet
                if net_tx(0, tx_buf_handle, sample_packet.len() as u64).is_ok() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Transmitted packet!");
                } else {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to transmit packet.");
                }

                // Free the allocated DMA buffer after transmission
                if net_free_buf(tx_buf_handle).is_err() {
                    log("Net-Bridge: Failed to free TX buffer.");
                }
                // --- End Simulate TX ---

            },
            Err(_) => {
                log("Net-Bridge: Error receiving IPC message on its channel.");
                // Potentially fatal error, depending on error handling strategy
            }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("Net-Bridge V-Node panicked!");
    // In a real system, attempt to free allocated resources like rx_buf_handle
    loop {}
}
'''

# For this interactive environment, we just print it to demonstrate the content.
print(rust_code)

"""

print("Creating vnode/net-bridge.rs code.")

### Subtask:
Define the vnode.yml for the 'svc://net-stack' V-Node, including its capabilities, runtime parameters, and its IPC API for communication with the 'net-bridge' V-Node and higher-level services.

**Reasoning**:
The user wants to define the `vnode.yml` for the `svc://net-stack` V-Node. This involves specifying metadata, runtime parameters, capabilities, and observability metrics relevant to a network stack. print("```yaml\n# vnode/net-stack/vnode.yml\nvnode:\n  name: \"aethernet-service\"\n  version: \"0.1.0\"\n  maintainer: \"aetheros-core-team@aetheros.org\"\n  mode: strict # A network stack is a critical system service\n\nruntime:\n  entrypoint: \"bin/aethernet-service.vnode\"\n  required_mem_mb: 64 # Enough for smoltcp, ARP cache, connection states\n  max_cpu_share: 0.10 # Can be CPU-intensive depending on traffic\n\ncapabilities:\n  - NetworkService # High-level capability for providing network services\n  - CAP_IPC_CONNECT: \"svc://net-bridge\" # Communicate with the net-bridge driver\n  - CAP_IPC_ACCEPT # Accept connections from higher-level V-Nodes (Socket API)\n  - CAP_MEM_SHARE # For zero-copy packet exchange with net-bridge\n  - CAP_TIME_READ # For internal smoltcp timers and RTT calculation\n\nstorage:\n  mounts:\n    - path: \"/etc/network\"\n      source: \"persistent://system-config/network\"\n      options: [ \"ro\" ]\n    - path: \"/var/run\"\n      source: \"volatile://ramdisk\"\n      size: \"1MB\" # For temporary files like DHCP leases\n\nobservability:\n  metrics: [\"packets_rx_total\", \"packets_tx_total\", \"connections_tcp_active\", \"arp_cache_entries\", \"latency_avg_ns\"]\n```")

"""**Reasoning**:
The next step is to sketch the main Rust code structure for the `svc://net-stack` V-Node, as per the subtask. This sketch will outline its main loop, `smoltcp` integration, IPC API, and `net-bridge` interaction.


"""

print("""```rust
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, E_ACC_DENIED};

// Placeholder for smoltcp types and functions
mod smoltcp_stub {
    #![allow(dead_code)]
    pub struct Interface; // Simplified smoltcp Interface
    pub struct Device; // Simplified smoltcp Device
    pub struct SocketSet; // Simplified smoltcp SocketSet

    impl Interface {
        pub fn poll(&mut self, _timestamp: u64, _device: &mut Device, _sockets: &mut SocketSet) {} // Simulate polling
        pub fn has_active_sockets(&self) -> bool { false }
    }
    impl Device {
        pub fn receive(&mut self, _buffer: &mut [u8]) -> Option<usize> { None } // Simulate RX
        pub fn transmit(&mut self, _buffer: &[u8]) -> Option<usize> { None } // Simulate TX
    }
    impl SocketSet { pub fn new() -> Self { SocketSet } }
    pub fn get_current_time_ns() -> u64 { 0 } // Simulate time
}

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// --- IPC API for other V-Nodes (Socket API) ---
// (Simplified for sketch, actual would use postcard serialization)
#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetStackRequest {
    OpenSocket(u32), // TCP/UDP, port
    Send(u32, Vec<u8>), // socket_handle, data
    Recv(u32), // socket_handle
    CloseSocket(u32), // socket_handle
}

#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetStackResponse {
    SocketOpened(u32), // socket_handle
    Data(Vec<u8>), // data
    Error(u32), // error_code
    Success,
}

// --- Communication with net-bridge Driver ---
// (Simplified, actual would use shared memory handles)
#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetBridgeRequest {
    GetRxPacket(u64), // DMA handle of buffer to fill
    SendTxPacket(u64, u64), // DMA handle, length
}

#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetBridgeResponse {
    PacketReceived(u64), // Length of packet received
    PacketSent, // Confirmation
    NoPacket,
}

// --- Main AetherNet Service Loop ---
#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For simplicity, hardcode channel ID for aethernet-service
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(3); // Channel to receive requests from other V-Nodes
    let mut bridge_chan = VNodeChannel::new(2); // Channel to communicate with net-bridge

    log("AetherNet Service V-Node starting up...");

    let mut smoltcp_iface = smoltcp_stub::Interface; // Initialize smoltcp interface
    let mut smoltcp_device = smoltcp_stub::Device; // Initialize smoltcp device
    let mut smoltcp_sockets = smoltcp_stub::SocketSet::new(); // Initialize smoltcp sockets

    // Main event loop for the network stack
    loop {
        let timestamp = smoltcp_stub::get_current_time_ns(); // Get current time for smoltcp polling

        // 1. Check for incoming packets from net-bridge
        // In a real scenario, net-bridge would send an IPC message when a packet is ready
        // For this sketch, we simulate polling net-bridge
        match bridge_chan.recv_blocking() { // Try to receive packet notification/data from net-bridge
            Ok(msg_data) => {
                // msg_data would contain info about packet from net-bridge
                log("AetherNet: Received packet notification from net-bridge.");
                // Request net-bridge to get the actual packet data (via shared memory/DMA handle)
                // For sketch, simulate receiving data directly.
                let mut rx_buffer = [0u8; 1536]; // Max Ethernet frame size
                if let Some(len) = smoltcp_device.receive(&mut rx_buffer) {
                    log("AetherNet: smoltcp_device.receive returned data.");
                    // Pass the received data to smoltcp interface
                    // smoltcp_iface.receive_frame(timestamp, &mut smoltcp_device, &mut smoltcp_sockets, &rx_buffer[..len]);
                }
                // Acknowledge IRQ to net-bridge if needed (not in this sketch)
            },
            Err(_) => { /* No message from net-bridge, continue */ }
        }

        // 2. Poll smoltcp interface for network events (e.g., ARP, ICMP, TCP/UDP activity)
        smoltcp_iface.poll(timestamp, &mut smoltcp_device, &mut smoltcp_sockets);

        // 3. Check if smoltcp has outgoing packets to send via net-bridge
        if smoltcp_iface.has_active_sockets() { // Simplified check for outgoing data
            let mut tx_buffer = [0u8; 1536];
            if let Some(len) = smoltcp_device.transmit(&mut tx_buffer) {
                log("AetherNet: smoltcp_device.transmit has outgoing data.");
                // Send the outgoing packet to net-bridge (via shared memory/DMA handle)
                // bridge_chan.send(&NetBridgeRequest::SendTxPacket(tx_buffer_handle, len as u64));
            }
        }

        // 4. Process incoming requests from other V-Nodes (Socket API)
        match own_chan.recv_blocking() {
            Ok(req_data) => {
                if let Ok(request) = postcard::from_bytes::<NetStackRequest>(&req_data) {
                    log("AetherNet: Received request from another V-Node.");
                    let response = match request {
                        NetStackRequest::OpenSocket(port) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Opening socket on port {}", port));
                            NetStackResponse::SocketOpened(port + 1000) // Simulate new handle
                        },
                        NetStackRequest::Send(handle, data) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Sending {} bytes on socket {}", data.len(), handle));
                            // Here, smoltcp would be used to send data
                            NetStackResponse::Success
                        },
                        NetStackRequest::Recv(handle) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Receiving on socket {}", handle));
                            // Here, smoltcp would be used to receive data
                            NetStackResponse::Data(Vec::new()) // Simulate empty data
                        },
                        NetStackRequest::CloseSocket(handle) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Closing socket {}", handle));
                            NetStackResponse::Success
                        },
                    };
                    own_chan.send(&response).unwrap_or_else(|_| log("AetherNet: Failed to send response."));
                } else {
                    log("AetherNet: Failed to deserialize NetStackRequest.");
                }
            },
            Err(_) => { /* No request from other V-Nodes, continue */ }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("AetherNet Service V-Node panicked!");
    loop {}
}
```"""

"""**Reasoning**:

The ````rust` markdown block was also not correctly closed within the Python string. I will fix the string termination by ensuring the Python triple quotes `'''` are correctly placed to close the literal, and the Rust markdown block is also properly closed.


"""

print("""```rust
#![no_std]
#![no_main]

extern crate alloc;

use core::panic::PanicInfo;
use alloc::vec::Vec;
use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall2, syscall3, SYS_LOG, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, E_ACC_DENIED};

// Placeholder for smoltcp types and functions
mod smoltcp_stub {
    #![allow(dead_code)]
    pub struct Interface; // Simplified smoltcp Interface
    pub struct Device; // Simplified smoltcp Device
    pub struct SocketSet; // Simplified smoltcp SocketSet

    impl Interface {
        pub fn poll(&mut self, _timestamp: u64, _device: &mut Device, _sockets: &mut SocketSet) {} // Simulate polling
        pub fn has_active_sockets(&self) -> bool { false }
    }
    impl Device {
        pub fn receive(&mut self, _buffer: &mut [u8]) -> Option<usize> { None } // Simulate RX
        pub fn transmit(&mut self, _buffer: &[u8]) -> Option<usize> { None } // Simulate TX
    }
    impl SocketSet { pub fn new() -> Self { SocketSet } }
    pub fn get_current_time_ns() -> u64 { 0 } // Simulate time
}

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// --- IPC API for other V-Nodes (Socket API) ---
// (Simplified for sketch, actual would use postcard serialization)
#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetStackRequest {
    OpenSocket(u32), // TCP/UDP, port
    Send(u32, Vec<u8>), // socket_handle, data
    Recv(u32), // socket_handle
    CloseSocket(u32), // socket_handle
}

#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetStackResponse {
    SocketOpened(u32), // socket_handle
    Data(Vec<u8>),
    Error(u32), // error_code
    Success,
}

// --- Communication with net-bridge Driver ---
// (Simplified, actual would use shared memory handles)
#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetBridgeRequest {
    GetRxPacket(u64), // DMA handle of buffer to fill
    SendTxPacket(u64, u64), // DMA handle, length
}

#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetBridgeResponse {
    PacketReceived(u64), // Length of packet received
    PacketSent, // Confirmation
    NoPacket,
}

// --- Main AetherNet Service Loop ---
#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
    // For simplicity, hardcode channel ID for aethernet-service
    let mut own_chan = VNodeChannel::new(3); // Channel to receive requests from other V-Nodes
    let mut bridge_chan = VNodeChannel::new(2); // Channel to communicate with net-bridge

    log("AetherNet Service V-Node starting up...");

    let mut smoltcp_iface = smoltcp_stub::Interface; // Initialize smoltcp interface
    let mut smoltcp_device = smoltcp_stub::Device; // Initialize smoltcp device
    let mut smoltcp_sockets = smoltcp_stub::SocketSet::new(); // Initialize smoltcp sockets

    // Main event loop for the network stack
    loop {
        let timestamp = smoltcp_stub::get_current_time_ns(); // Get current time for smoltcp polling

        // 1. Check for incoming packets from net-bridge
        // In a real scenario, net-bridge would send an IPC message when a packet is ready
        // For this sketch, we simulate polling net-bridge
        match bridge_chan.recv_blocking() { // Try to receive packet notification/data from net-bridge
            Ok(msg_data) => {
                // msg_data would contain info about packet from net-bridge
                log("AetherNet: Received packet notification from net-bridge.");
                // Request net-bridge to get the actual packet data (via shared memory/DMA handle)
                // For sketch, simulate receiving data directly.
                let mut rx_buffer = [0u8; 1536]; // Max Ethernet frame size
                if let Some(len) = smoltcp_device.receive(&mut rx_buffer) {
                    log("AetherNet: smoltcp_device.receive returned data.");
                    // Pass the received data to smoltcp interface
                    // smoltcp_iface.receive_frame(timestamp, &mut smoltcp_device, &mut smoltcp_sockets, &rx_buffer[..len]);
                }
                // Acknowledge IRQ to net-bridge if needed (not in this sketch)
            },
            Err(_) => { /* No message from net-bridge, continue */ }
        }

        // 2. Poll smoltcp interface for network events (e.g., ARP, ICMP, TCP/UDP activity)
        smoltcp_iface.poll(timestamp, &mut smoltcp_device, &mut smoltcp_sockets);

        // 3. Check if smoltcp has outgoing packets to send via net-bridge
        if smoltcp_iface.has_active_sockets() { // Simplified check for outgoing data
            let mut tx_buffer = [0u8; 1536];
            if let Some(len) = smoltcp_device.transmit(&mut tx_buffer) {
                log("AetherNet: smoltcp_device.transmit has outgoing data.");
                // Send the outgoing packet to net-bridge (via shared memory/DMA handle)
                // bridge_chan.send(&NetBridgeRequest::SendTxPacket(tx_buffer_handle, len as u64));
            }
        }

        // 4. Process incoming requests from other V-Nodes (Socket API)
        match own_chan.recv_blocking() {
            Ok(req_data) => {
                if let Ok(request) = postcard::from_bytes::<NetStackRequest>(&req_data) {
                    log("AetherNet: Received request from another V-Node.");
                    let response = match request {
                        NetStackRequest::OpenSocket(port) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Opening socket on port {}", port));
                            NetStackResponse::SocketOpened(port + 1000) // Simulate new handle
                        },
                        NetStackRequest::Send(handle, data) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Sending {} bytes on socket {}", data.len(), handle));
                            // Here, smoltcp would be used to send data
                            NetStackResponse::Success
                        },
                        NetStackRequest::Recv(handle) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Receiving on socket {}", handle));
                            // Here, smoltcp would be used to receive data
                            NetStackResponse::Data(Vec::new()) // Simulate empty data
                        },
                        NetStackRequest::CloseSocket(handle) => {
                            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNet: Closing socket {}", handle));
                            NetStackResponse::Success
                        },
                    };
                    own_chan.send(&response).unwrap_or_else(|_| log("AetherNet: Failed to send response."));
                } else {
                    log("AetherNet: Failed to deserialize NetStackRequest.");
                }
            },
            Err(_) => { /* No request from other V-Nodes, continue */ }
        }
    }
}

#[panic_handler]
fn panic(_info: &PanicInfo) -> ! {
    log("AetherNet Service V-Node panicked!");
    loop {}
}
```""")

"""## Design AetherNet V-Node (svc://net-stack)

### Subtask:
Integrate smoltcp into the 'svc://net-stack' V-Node to provide actual network protocol logic.

**Reasoning**:
The subtask requires integrating `smoltcp` into the `svc://net-stack` V-Node. This involves defining `smoltcp` types and implementing the `smoltcp::phy::Device` trait for `AetherNetDevice` to handle RX/TX with `net-bridge` via IPC and DMA handles. I'll start by providing the Rust code for `vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs` which will include necessary `smoltcp` imports and the `AetherNetDevice` implementation, along with placeholder IPC interactions with the `net-bridge` driver.
"""

print("Creating vnode/net-stack/aethernet_device.rs for smoltcp device implementation.")

rust_code = r'''
#![no_std]

extern crate alloc;

use alloc::vec::Vec;
use smoltcp::phy::{Device, RxToken, TxToken, Checksum, DeviceCapabilities};
use smoltcp::time::Instant;
use smoltcp::wire::{EthernetAddress, HardwareAddress};

use crate::ipc::vnode::VNodeChannel;
use crate::syscall::{syscall3, SYS_LOG, SUCCESS, E_ERROR, SYS_NET_ALLOC_BUF, SYS_NET_FREE_BUF, SYS_NET_TX, SYS_NET_RX_POLL, get_dma_buffer_ptr};

// Temporary log function for V-Nodes
fn log(msg: &str) {
    unsafe {
        let res = syscall3(
            SYS_LOG,
            msg.as_ptr() as u64,
            msg.len() as u64,
            0 // arg3 is unused for SYS_LOG
        );
        if res != SUCCESS { /* Handle log error, maybe panic or fall back */ }
    }
}

// IPC API for communication with net-bridge (simplified for device trait)
#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetBridgeRequest {
    // For smoltcp Device trait, direct syscalls to kernel are more appropriate
    // as net-bridge is a driver that exposes low-level packet handling via kernel syscalls.
    // Higher level IPC with net-bridge would be for control plane, not data plane.
}

#[derive(Debug)]
enum NetBridgeResponse {
    // No direct responses expected for data plane during RX/TX from net-bridge
}

/// Represents a single received packet buffer for smoltcp.
pub struct PacketRxToken<'a> {
    buffer: &'a mut [u8],
    dma_handle: u64,
}

impl<'a> RxToken for PacketRxToken<'a> {
    fn consume<R, F>(self, _timestamp: Instant, f: F) -> R
    where
        F: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> R,
    {
        // The smoltcp stack consumes the packet data
        let result = f(self.buffer);
        // After consumption, free the DMA buffer
        if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(self.dma_handle) {
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free RX DMA buffer: {}", e));
        }
        result
    }
}

/// Represents a single transmitted packet buffer for smoltcp.
pub struct PacketTxToken<'a> {
    buffer: &'a mut [u8],
    dma_handle: u64,
    len: usize,
    iface_id: u64,
}

impl<'a> TxToken for PacketTxToken<'a> {
    fn consume<R, F>(self, _timestamp: Instant, f: F) -> R
    where
        F: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> R,
    {
        let result = f(self.buffer);
        // After smoltcp fills the buffer, transmit it
        if let Err(e) = net_tx(self.iface_id, self.dma_handle, self.len as u64) {
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to transmit packet: {}", e));
        }
        // Free the DMA buffer after transmission
        if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(self.dma_handle) {
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free TX DMA buffer: {}", e));
        }
        result
    }
}

/// AetherNetDevice implements smoltcp::phy::Device for communication with net-bridge V-Node.
pub struct AetherNetDevice {
    iface_id: u64, // Interface ID, typically 0 for the first NIC
    bridge_chan: VNodeChannel, // Channel to net-bridge for control/notifications if needed
}

impl AetherNetDevice {
    pub fn new(iface_id: u64, channel_id: u32) -> Self {
        AetherNetDevice {
            iface_id,
            bridge_chan: VNodeChannel::new(channel_id),
        }
    }
}

impl<'a> Device<'a> for AetherNetDevice {
    type RxToken = PacketRxToken<'a>;
    type TxToken = PacketTxToken<'a>;

    fn capabilities(&self) -> DeviceCapabilities {
        let mut caps = DeviceCapabilities::default();
        caps.max_transmission_unit = 1500;
        caps.max_burst_size = Some(1);
        caps.checksum = Checksum::None;
        caps.medium = smoltcp::phy::Medium::Ethernet;
        caps
    }

    fn receive(&'a mut self, _timestamp: Instant) -> Option<(Self::RxToken, Self::TxToken)> {
        // Allocate a DMA buffer for incoming packet
        let dma_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
            Ok(h) => h,
            Err(e) => { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to alloc RX DMA buffer: {}", e)); return None; }
        };

        // Poll the kernel for a received packet
        let len = unsafe {
            syscall3(
                SYS_NET_RX_POLL,
                self.iface_id, // Interface ID
                dma_handle, // DMA handle where kernel should copy data
                1536 as u64 // Max buffer length
            )
        };

        if len > SUCCESS { // len > 0 indicates a packet was received
            if let Some(buf_ptr) = get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
                let buffer = unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(buf_ptr, len as usize) };
                // For smoltcp, we must return both RxToken and an optional TxToken.
                // In many cases, we don't immediately transmit on receive, so TxToken can be None.
                // For simplicity, we just return the RxToken here.
                Some((PacketRxToken { buffer, dma_handle }, PacketTxToken {
                    buffer: &mut [], // dummy buffer
                    dma_handle: 0,
                    len: 0,
                    iface_id: self.iface_id
                }))
            } else {
                log("AetherNetDevice: Failed to get buffer pointer for RX DMA handle.");
                if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free RX DMA buffer (ptr error): {}", e)); }
                None
            }
        } else if len == SUCCESS { // No packet or spurious IRQ
             // Free the unused buffer immediately if no packet was received
             if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free unused RX DMA buffer: {}", e)); }
            None
        } else { // Error from syscall
            log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: SYS_NET_RX_POLL returned error code: {}", len));
             if let Err(e) = net_free_buf(dma_handle) { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to free RX DMA buffer (syscall error): {}", e)); }
            None
        }
    }

    fn transmit(&'a mut self, _timestamp: Instant) -> Option<Self::TxToken> {
        // Allocate a DMA buffer for outgoing packet
        let dma_handle = match net_alloc_buf(1536) {
            Ok(h) => h,
            Err(e) => { log(&alloc::format!("AetherNetDevice: Failed to alloc TX DMA buffer: {}", e)); return None; }
        };

        if let Some(buf_ptr) = get_dma_buffer_ptr(dma_handle) {
